After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit:https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

March 11, 2020 
Dear Mr. Kiper:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and 
then think of non-urgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel free 
to use the attached questionnaire to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff member 
or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we 
hope you will take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your 
feedback to improve our patient care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare 
emergency department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

This test has not been cleared or approved for diagnostic use by the
U.S. Food and Drug Administration. This test has been authorized by
FDA under an EUA for use by authorized laboratories. This test has
been authorized only for the detection of RNA from SARS-CoV-2 virus
and diagnosis of SARS-CoV-2 virus infection, not for any other
viruses or pathogens. This test is only authorized for the duration
of the declaration that circumstances exist justifying the
authorization of the emergency use of in vitro diagnostic tests for
detection of SARS-CoV-2 virus and/or diagnosis of SARS-CoV-2 virus
infection under section 564(b)(1) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. section
360bbb-3(b)(1), unless the authorization is terminated or revoked
sooner.
We will continue to follow federal and state requirements for both
notification of results and any confirmatory testing that is
required by another agency.
This test was developed and its performance characteristics

recovery from acute blood loss, hemolytic anemia, kidney disease,
or the presence of unstable hemoglobins like HbSS, HbCC, and HbSC
may yield falsely decreased HbA1c test results. Iron deficiency may
yield falsely increased HbA1c test results.

Anti-SARS-Cov-2 IgG Antibody -- Negative — N
Comment: 
(note)
This test has not been reviewed by the FDA. Negative results do 
not rule out SARS-CoV-2 infection, particularly in those who have 
been in contact with the virus. Follow-up testing with a molecular 
diagnostic should be considered to rule out infection in these 
individuals. Results from antibody testing should not be used as 
the sole basis to diagnose or exclude SARS-CoV-2 infection or to 
inform infection status. Positive results may be due to past or 
present infection with non-SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus strains, such as 
coronavirus HKU1, NL63, OC43, or 229E. Not for the screening of 
donated blood.
Result: 
Negative
The purpose of the serology test is to detect antibodies to SARS-CoV-2, the virus that causes COVID-19. This test is not 
meant to diagnose COVID-19.
Your negative result means that antibodies to SARS-CoV-2 were not detected in the specimen that was tested and/or you 
did not develop a detectable immune response to COVID-19.

Dear Mr. Kiper:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and then think of nonurgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you.Feel free to use the attached questionnaire 
to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent 
questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we hope you will 
take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your feedback to improve our patient 
care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare emergency 
department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Chief Complaint
Patient presents with
• Cyst
SUBJECTIVE
James Kiper is a 44 yr/o male who presents to the office today with concern of a cyst on his buttocks. Patient relates 
that he has been sitting around a lot since he was laid off work with the coronavirus. He also relates he was on his 
mother's roof repairing it for about 15 minutes and noted a cystic-like growth on the buttocks between the cheeks. It 
apparently it hurts significantly 3 days ago now it is resolving

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN 
James was seen today for cyst.
Diagnoses and all orders for this visit:
Pilonidal cyst
Hypothyroidism, unspecified type
Patient was instructed to soak his buttocks 20 to 30 minutes a day. There is no abscess formation or carbuncle. This 
may represent a small cyst however it appears to be smaller and it is nontender I would recommend conservative 
therapy continue the soaks on a daily basis

Kiper, James (MRN EP00479698) DOB: [DOB-7230] Encounter Date: [DOB-4757]

Physical Examination:
Vitals: BP 120/70 (BP Location: Left arm, Orthostatic Position: Sitting) | Pulse 84 | Temp 97.9
°F (36.6 ”C) | Ht 5' 8.5" (174 cm) | Wt 65.8 kg (145 lb) |Sp02 98% | BMI 21.73 kg/m2
General appearance: Well nourished, well developed and well groomed., appears stated age
Eyes: pupils are equal, conjunctivae are pink, sclera are non-icteric
ENT: ears and nose normal, mucous membranes are moist, hearing is normal
Neck: supple, symmetric
Respiratory: unlabored respirations
Buttocks: Fluctuant area left and lateral to anal orifice on left buttock, slightly tender to touch,
minimal discharge
Musculoskeletal: no cyanosis, muscle tone is normal
Skin: no rashes or diaphoresis
Psychiatric: Oriented to person and place, no distress
Impression:
44 yr/o male with evidence of a perianal abscess on the left buttock. This does not appear to
be completely drained.
Plan:
Options are discussed with the patient. I am recommending proceeding with incision and
drainage of left perirectal abscess. Generalities of the procedure were described. Risks
including but not limited to bleeding, infection, and need for additional procedures were
discussed. The patient indicates understanding and agreement to the above plan.
Description of procedure:
The patient was placed lateral on the stretcher. Attention was turned to the left buttock. Skin
was prepped with ChloraPrep and then anesthetized with 1% lidocaine with epinephrine. A
radial incision was created using #11 scalpel. Purulent material was drained. The wound was
packed with packing gauze. He was given wound care instructions. Plan to follow-up in 1
week.
Gregory B. Strothman, MD

Progress Notes
Cosicined by. Strothman,Gregory B., MD at 4/27/2021 1:47 PM
Date: 4/27/2021
Patient: James Kiper
DOB: 4/29/1976
Romines, Aliie R, PA-C (Physician Assistant) •Physician Assistant-Surgery
Subjective:
Subjective
James Kiper presents to the office for a scheduled post-op visit. He is 7 days following incision
and drainage of left perirectal abscess in office. The patient is tolerating a regular diet
without difficulty. Bowel movements are Normal. He has not had much improvement in pain. He
says initially the pressure was relieved after l&D, but has started to feel pressure sensation
again. He is not having any fever or chills. There has been moderate, purulent drainage from
the incision. He is unable to pack the wound because it is too small and just falls out. He has to
change his bandages three times a day.
The following portions of the patient's history were reviewed and updated as appropriate:
allergies, current medications, past family history, past medical history, past social history, past
surgical history and problem list.
Objective:
Objective

BMI 21.73 kg/m2
General: alert and no distress
Buttocks/IncE/o anal fistula with open tract located left and lateral to anal orifice on left
ision: buttock, draining purulent fluid
Assessment:
S/P incision and drainage of left perirectal abscess in office. Reports persistent drainage and
pressure like sensation, with finding of anal fistula on physical exam.

Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Above-noted
Called patient to inform another urgent referral was placed for this perianal abscess, patient states it is very tender, 
raw from the drainage, and just cannot take the discomfort any longer, and the drainage. This has been going on for 
over 40 days patient states and needs to get in to see Gastroenterologist regarding this abscess. PCP informed 
patient scheduled with him for this afternoon to evaluate changes, and increased pain. PCP had sent patient to Dr. 
Strothman whom stated this patient needs to see Gastro.
Order queued for PCP to review/authorize

Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.

Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor

After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

Patient Instructions
1. Continue all current medications.
2. Your prescriptions will be electronically sent to your pharmacy. Take a probiotic such as generic Acidophilus or 
Lactobacillus tablets, Align, Equate, or Phillips name brand tablets, or Activia yogurt once everyday while on the 
antibiotics.
3. Please go to MyCharts or call the office [PHONE-5183]) to arrange your next office appointment.
4. We encourage you to follow these recommendations:
 1. Exercise regularly (3 hours per week minimum) and normalize your weight (BMI of less than 25).
2. See the eye doctor every two years after age 40.
 3. Take your medications regularly as directed, and see your physician every 3-6 months ( or more often if 
instructed by doctor) for regular follow-up.
 4. We want your blood pressure to run below 120/80.

Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor

MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results,allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

antibiotic for that. Patient is allergic to many antibiotics - Ciprofloxacin, Doxy, PCNS and Sulfa. Patient recently had 
Surgery on anal fistula and states that is the worse part about all the coughing. Please advise, as you have no work in 
appts and patient does not want to sit in waiting room of ICC secondary to discomfort with sitting at this time. Please 
advise, thank you! 
Please inform patient we cannot treat based on a home test. He needs to be seen by a provider and tested or proceed 
to ICC. 
Patient states he may have upper respiratory infection due to coughing up green mucus in chest and needing a call 
back
Caller / patient states the following Medical Question: 
Patient calling because he tested positive for COVID using at home test on Saturday. He wants to know if he needs to 
retest this week. He also wants to know what his treatment options are. 
He has low fever, cough, congestion, body aches. 
Advised Caller: - a message would be sent to the office with their request.

Summary History: 
James Kiper is a 45 yr/o male with has a past medical history of Allergy history unknown, Blood transfusion without 
reported diagnosis, Cough, Hypothyroidism, Other diseases of nasal cavity and sinuses(478.19), and Strain of neck 
(7/2015).
Here with concern of episode of dizziness - this morning he had an episode of dizziness, with heavy eyelids, vision out 
of focus and some frontal head pressure. After drinking some water he felt better and dizziness resolved. The day 
before he'd experienced abdominal bloating and headache. 
ROS: - no vertigo or falls
- no memory lapses
- no known sz activity
- no chest pain or palpitations
+ mild nausea

Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101,0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.

MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results,allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

Please contact our office for any questions

recent TSH was 14.25. Patient relates that he is taking Synthroid 100 mcg on a daily basis. He does not take it 
fasting however.

the skin surface

Clinical Notes (continued)
avoiding the need to cut the sphincter muscle loose setons allow fistulas to drain but not cure them setons can be 
used alone or with a fistulectomy in a staged procedure the seton helps the fistula heal from the inside out this may be 
needed before final surgery 3 repair of the fistula at which time the seton is removed. Setons may need to be changed 
every 4 to 6 weeks in the outpatient setting. There is no specific length of time the seton is maintained some we will 
keep it for 6 months or longer

Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.

MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results,allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

Clinical Notes

back in if this still occurred. Patient stated that his foot, ankles and all joints are all hurting off an on for the last month.

MD for today at 1 pm. Patient voiced understanding and was appreciative of this call.

Clinical Notes
Subjective:
Subjective Patient ID: James Kiper is a 46 yr/o male.
Abdominal Pain
This is a recurrent problem. The current episode started more than 1 month ago. The problem occurs daily. The 
problem has been waxing and waning. The pain is located in the RLQ. The pain is mild. The quality of the pain is 
aching. The abdominal pain radiates to the LLQ. Associated symptoms include arthralgias. Pertinent negatives include

no anorexia, constipation or fever. The pain is aggravated by certain positions and palpation. The pain is relieved by

Assessment and Plan:
Assessment
James was seen today for office visit.
Diagnoses and all orders for this visit:
Strain of abdominal wall, subsequent encounter
Arthralgia, unspecified joint
recurrent rlq pain, reassurance provided. This is likely muscular strain. Symptoms reside over his scar from remote 
appendectomy; counseled to monitor symptoms and let us know if no better. Remains very active, recommended rest 
to allow healing and then increase activity as tolerated which he is agreeable to.

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Chief Complaint
Patient presents with
• Motor Vehicle Crash
Was rear ended on Monday went to the ER the took Xrays and told him to follow up with pcp 
complains of lower,middle back pain and neck pain 
SUBJECTIVE
James Kiper is a 46 yr/o male who presents to the office today for a follow-up of recent MVA. The patient was 
involved in a hit-and-run. He was rear-ended which sustained injury to his neck thoracic and lumbar spine. This 
occurred on Monday. Patient relates that he has more stiffness and pain each day. He was seen in the emergency 
department St. Mary's where x-rays were performed. Patient was told they were negative.
Today the patient is stiff and has a moderate degree of muscle guarding.
Patient remains up-to-date with thyroid replacement therapy for hypothyroidism. He takes Synthroid 25 mcg daily.
ROS
Review of Systems
No fever chills no PND
MEDICATIONS
Current Outpatient Medications

tablet

Clinical Notes (continued)
ASSESSMENT AND PLAN 
James was seen today for motor vehicle crash.
Diagnoses and all orders for this visit:
Acute strain of neck muscle, initial encounter
-CT Head Wo Contrast; Future
Spasm of thoracic back muscle
-CT Head Wo Contrast; Future
Spasm of lumbar paraspinous muscle
-CT Head Wo Contrast; Future
Hypothyroidism, unspecified type
Chronic rectal pain
Anal fistula
Other orders
-baclofen (LIORESAL) 10 MG tablet; Take 1 tablet by mouth 3 (three) times daily as needed (muscle spasm)
for up to 14 days.
Patient has moderate pain associated with stiffness in the neck and upper and mid back. The patient was involved in 
a rapid the accelerating type of injury. He was struck from behind. The patient was restrained at the time. He does 
have a mild headache as well.
Patient has ibuprofen 800 mg he was instructed to use this up to 3 times a day for relief of pain.
In view of the headache and the accelerating injury the patient will have blood work as well as a noncontrast CT of the 
head.
Patient has a seton stitch on both left and right buttocks use to aid in the healing of the fistula.
CHIEF COMPLAINT
Chief Complaint
Patient presents with
• Motor Vehicle Crash
Was rear ended on Monday went to the ER the took Xrays and told him to follow up with pcp 
complains of lower,middle back pain and neck pain

[DOB-5984] - Office Visit in Norton Community Medical Associates - Audubon (continued)
Clinical Notes (continued)
SUBJECTIVE
James Kiper is a 46 yr/o male who presents to the office today for a follow-up of recent MVA. The patient was 
involved in a hit-and-run. He was rear-ended which sustained injury to his neck thoracic and lumbar spine. This 
occurred on Monday. Patient relates that he has more stiffness and pain each day. He was seen in the emergency 
department St. Mary's where x-rays were performed. Patient was told they were negative.
Today the patient is stiff and has a moderate degree of muscle guarding.
Patient remains up-to-date with thyroid replacement therapy for hypothyroidism. He takes Synthroid 25 mcg daily.
ROS
Review of Systems
No fever chills no PND
MEDICATIONS
Current Outpatient Medications
Medication Sig Dispense Refill • Bacillus Coagulans-Inulin 
(PROBIOTIC) 1-250 BILLIONMG CAPSCap, Oral, Daily, 0 
Refill(s) • baclofen (LIORESAL) 10 MG 
tablet

mouth 3 (three) times 
daily as needed 
(muscle spasm) for 
up to 14 days.

Constitutional: 
HENT: Normocephalic, Atraumatic, Bilateral external ears normal, Oropharynx moist, No oral exudates, Nose normal.

all major joints. No tenderness to palpation or major deformities noted. Back- No tenderness.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN 
James was seen today for motor vehicle crash.
Diagnoses and all orders for this visit:
Acute strain of neck muscle, initial encounter
-CT Head Wo Contrast; Future
Spasm of thoracic back muscle
-CT Head Wo Contrast; Future
Spasm of lumbar paraspinous muscle
-CT Head Wo Contrast; Future
Other orders
-baclofen (LIORESAL) 10 MG tablet; Take 1 tablet by mouth 3 (three) times daily as needed (muscle spasm)
for up to 14 days.
Patient has moderate pain associated with stiffness in the neck and upper and mid back. The patient was involved in 
a rapid the accelerating type of injury. He was struck from behind. The patient was restrained at the time. He does 
have a mild headache as well.
Patient has ibuprofen 800 mg he was instructed to use this up to 3 times a day for relief of pain.
In view of the headache and the accelerating injury the patient will have blood work as well as a noncontrast CT of the 
head.

Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.

Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Attempted to call patient to inform that he is correct. PCP cancelled CT head, CT neck. PCP ordered MRI of C spine 
and L spine, instead. Left detailed message, informing patient. Asked patient to call office back with any other 
questions. WMLPN 11:44 am. 
Per encounter below, pt knows this information, but wants to just make sure that with the MRI that was done, he does 
not need a CT of head correct? Please call him to clarify. He has received calls to scheduled CT scans so he is 
confused and not sure if it is necessary. 
 Queued new MRI orders as directed by PCP per patient request, secondary to cost/insurance. Discontinued CT 
order for CT neck/Ct head at direction of PCP. Notified patient on their NMC with PCP's response to (his) patient's 
request.

Caller / patient states the following Medical Question: - Patient states was seen today for MVA and that pcp placed an order today for a CT of his neck and head.States
that he also has an issue with his back and states that the facility that he is at can do an MRI of his back. He is 
wanting to know if PCP wants him to have the MRI with the facility he is at with Mary L Barresi DO Synergy Injury 
Center and then do the CT with Norton? Please call patient and advise of what PCP would want him to do Call back 
patient [PHONE-6065]. Facility # [PHONE-6066]

Clinical Notes
Subjective:
Subjective Patient ID: James Kiper is a 46 yr/o male.
Sinusitis
This is a recurrent problem. The current episode started 1 to 4 weeks ago. The problem is unchanged. There has been 
no fever. The pain is moderate. Associated symptoms include congestion, coughing, headaches, a hoarse voice and 
sinus pressure. Pertinent negatives include no chills, diaphoresis or shortness of breath. Past treatments include oral 
decongestants and antibiotics. The treatment provided mild relief.

If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call,letter or MyChart message regarding

Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101 0”F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor

MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results,allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

[DOB-6412] - Office Visit in Norton Community Medical Associates - Audubon (continued)
Clinical Notes (continued)
Bone marrow signal throughout the pelvis is within normal limits. No abnormal enhancement of the bone marrow. No 
intrapelvic mass or adenopathy. No significant free fluid. Heterogeneity of the prostate. Urinary bladder unremarkable. 
Suspect linear defect at the posterior anus at the midline. Linear sinus tract extending to the left of midline as well as 
the right of midline from the defect to the skin surface at the gluteal cleft inferiorly. No sizable fluid collection. There is 
some susceptibility artifact suggesting either soft tissue gas or findings related to prior intervention. Tiny bilateral 
scrotal hydroceles are noted. 
IMPRESSION: Anal fissure suspected at the posterior midline with linear sinus tract extending bilaterally to the skin 
surface at the inferior gluteal cleft consistent with fistulas. No sizable drainable abscess. 
Concerned regarding comment noting heterogeneity of prostate; he reports some intermittent testicle pain that is 
usually mild in nature but no other urinary complaints; concerned about prostate cancer and would like to consider 
additional testing and possible urology consultation; 
Also experiencing intermittent palpitations; had been monitoring his thyroid levels; last check was normal
Lab Results
 Component Value Date 
 TSH 2.250 [DOB-6413] 
The following portions of the patient's history were reviewed and updated as appropriate: allergies, current 
medications, past family history, past medical history, past social history, past surgical history and problem list.

change. 
Eyes: Negative for visual disturbance. 
Respiratory: Negative for shortness of breath and wheezing. 
Cardiovascular: Positive for palpitations. Negative for chest pain and leg swelling. 
Gastrointestinal: Positive for rectal pain. Negative for abdominal pain. 
 Sees colorectal at UofL regarding anal fissures
Genitourinary: Negative for difficulty urinating. 
Musculoskeletal: Negative for arthralgias. 
Skin: Negative for color change and rash. 
Neurological: Negative for weakness, light-headedness and numbness.

Your Medication List as of June 15, 2023 9:48 AM
® Always use your most recent med list.
levothyroxine 125 MCG tablet
Commonly known as: SYNTHROID
For diagnoses: Hypothyroidism, unspecified type
Take 1 tablet by mouth daily.
Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic

Clinical Notes
CHIEF COMPLAINT
Chief Complaint
Patient presents with
• Hypothyroidism
He is still having palpitations and he is wanting his thyroid rechecked • Annual Exam
SUBJECTIVE
James Kiper is a 47 yr/o male who presents to the office today for annual exam. The patient is taking his 
levothyroxine 125 mcg daily. In the past he was taking it 6 days a week.
Patient has had a right-sided seton in the past no major abscesses. He has had 6 surgeries patient continues to have 
drainage from both sides.
The patient was found to have 2 tiny external openings with small amount of subcutaneous pus. There was concern 
of a anal fistula
Anal fissure was suspected at the midline with linear sinus tract extending bilaterally to the skin surface at the inferior 
gluteal cleft consistent with fistulous no sizable drainage was identified this was performed on 6/27/2023 by Sandra 
Lee Kavalukas
ROS
Review of Systems
MEDICATIONS
Current Outpatient Medications
Medication Sig Dispense Refill • Alum & Mag Hydroxide-Simeth 
(MAALOX) 200-200-20 
MG/5ML suspension
Take by mouth every 
6 (six) hours as 
needed. • Ibuprofen 200 MG CAPS

Patient is status post a horseshoe fistula, status post posterior midline seton placement with slow recovery
During the of the visit the patient was educated and counseled about appropriate screening and preventive services. 
The following written plan was given to the patient.
1b. Get a dental checkup annually
2a. Exercise, aim for at least 20 minutes, 3 times a week
4a. Limit alcohol to less than 10 drinks a week. A drink is one beer, 4 ounces of wine, or 1.5 ounces of liquor.
5. Wear you safety belt
6i. Nutrition: look into ways to improve your diet, consider nutrition counseling.
6l. Medications: get help managing your medicine, consider using a weekly pillbox, or having someone help you 
arrange your pills for you.
6n. Sleep: improve your sleep habits, reduce sleeping medications
11b. Over the counter pain medications should be limited. They can cause stomach bleeding, and kidney or liver 
damage.
16b. Screening Test-Colonoscopy soul be preformed at least every 10 years until age 75 (or stool test more 
frequently) for Colorectal cancer. 
16f. Shots- get a Flu vaccine annually.
16j. Shots- get a Tetanus vaccine every 10 years.

Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.ora
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.

Patient stated that his foot is swollen under the big toe where the toe and foot connect. Has tired icing the foot and 
taking ibuprofen. Denied any injury. Stated that the foot mainly sore and has been swollen for the last week and half. 
Reports that its his left foot. Denied any redness, stated that the foot is only tender where swollen. Scheduled with 
APRN for today at 1220. Was apprecative of this call.
Per encounter below pt is calling back to speak with a nurse to be worked in. I attempted to contact the office but did 
not get an answer. Please call pt back asap. 
Left message to return call.

has helped

Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Patient ID: James Kiper is a 47 yr/o male.
Chief Complaint: Chief Complaint
Patient presents with
• Foot Swelling
Pt reports of left foot soreness and swelling 
Left foot pain - worse in am, better as day goes on
Foot Pain
This is a new problem. The current episode started 1 to 4 weeks ago. The problem occurs constantly. The problem 
has been unchanged. Associated symptoms include arthralgias, joint swelling and myalgias. Pertinent negatives 
include no abdominal pain, anorexia, change in bowel habit, chest pain, chills, congestion, coughing, diaphoresis, 
fatigue, fever, headaches, nausea, neck pain, numbness, rash, sore throat, swollen glands, urinary symptoms, vertigo, 
visual change, vomiting or weakness. The symptoms are aggravated by standing. He has tried NSAIDs, ice and rest 
for the symptoms. The treatment provided mild relief.

• Bilateral myopia [DOB-6663]
• Central serous chorioretinopathy of left eye [DOB-6664]

[DOB-6681] - Office Visit in Norton Community Medical Associates - Audubon (continued)
Clinical Notes (continued)
Cautioned to take mobic with food & avoid NSAIDS with it. 
Patient Instructions
Joint Pain:
1. If xrays were performed - results will be given via a phone call or mychart
2. Ice to area as often as possible - 20 min on/20 min off
3. Elevate area above the area of the heart
4. Compression sleeve that you can buy over the counter can provide support and help with pain - only wear while 
active during the day unless instructed otherwise
5. Tylenol/motrin/aleve as indicated on the bottle
6. Topicals such as icy-hot, biofreeze or tiger balm
7. Stop smoking, if applicable
8. Physical Therapy may be required to help you gain as much function back as possible - if referred, they will contact 
you
9. Specialist referral may be required for surgery, injections, or other non-surgical options. If referred, they will contact 
you
10. Opioid medications are not indicated for minor acute or chronic joint problems. This office will not prescribe them. 
Pain management referral can be arranged for chronic pain, but no guarantee of medication therapy will be given.
11. If the joint is swollen, red, hot to touch, or you have a fever > 101 - please contact the office or go to the 
emergency room 
Ref: to podiatry - they will call to schedule
Return for Keep appt in July with PCP.
Dr. Cassandra Lee, DNP, FNP-C, APRN
Family Practice
NCMA - Audubon

Patient Instructions
Joint Pain:
1. If xrays were performed - results will be given via a phone call or mychart
2. Ice to area as often as possible - 20 min on/20 min off
3. Elevate area above the area of the heart
4. Compression sleeve that you can buy over the counter can provide support and help with pain - only wear while 
active during the day unless instructed otherwise
5. Tylenol/motrin/aleve as indicated on the bottle
6. Topicals such as icy-hot, biofreeze or tiger balm
7. Stop smoking, if applicable
8. Physical Therapy may be required to help you gain as much function back as possible - if referred, they will contact 
you
9. Specialist referral may be required for surgery, injections, or other non-surgical options. If referred, they will contact 
you
10. Opioid medications are not indicated for minor acute or chronic joint problems. This office will not prescribe them. 
Pain management referral can be arranged for chronic pain, but no guarantee of medication therapy will be given.
11. If the joint is swollen, red, hot to touch, or you have a fever > 101 - please contact the office or go to the 
emergency room

Stopped Medication Instructions
Contact your pharmacy to notify of any stopped medications so they can stop any auto-refills or charges for
those medications.
Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call,letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.

MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit:https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Patient stated that he had his appendix removed, has scaring form this. Stated that believes that he has aggravated 
the old scar. Stated that the pain is on the right side above the hip, at belly button level. Pain is a ache or muscle ache. 
Has not lifted any thing heavy, or any injury. Stated that the pain has been on going for about a week but the last 3-4 
days the pain has not subsided. Did try a heating pad and this seems to help, did take a muscle relaxer last night and 
believes that this helped. Scheduled with APRN for 1/24 at 240 pm. Was apprecative of this call. 
Caller / patient is requesting an appointment: 
To treat the following symptoms: Right side pain
The situation began 2 weeks ago.
Caller / patient requests to be seen asap.
Advised Caller: - a message would be sent to the office with their request. - No appointments were available within the requested timeframe. - Provider's availability did not fit patient's needs.
Barriers to Scheduling:
- caller / patient needed an appointment sooner than next available on 4/2.
Caller Expected Outcome: - a phone call back.
All contact information has been validated and/or updated in the contact section of this encounter.

Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Patient ID: James Kiper is a 47 yr/o male.
Chief Complaint: Chief Complaint
Patient presents with
• Flank Pain
Right side started a week ago and comes and goes.
PT presents with L sided abd pain
Abdominal Pain
This is a new problem. The current episode started 1 to 4 weeks ago. The onset quality is sudden. The problem occurs 
daily. The problem has been unchanged. The pain is located in the RLQ and suprapubic region. The pain is at a 
severity of 2/10. The pain is mild. The quality of the pain is dull and aching. The abdominal pain radiates to the pelvis, 
scrotum and perineum. Associated symptoms include constipation. Pertinent negatives include no anorexia, 
arthralgias, belching, diarrhea, dysuria, fever, flatus, frequency, headaches, hematochezia, hematuria, melena, 
myalgias, nausea, vomiting or weight loss. The pain is aggravated by certain positions and movement. The pain is 
relieved by nothing. He has tried nothing for the symptoms. The treatment provided no relief. Prior diagnostic workup 
includes surgery.

Clinical Notes (continued)
Assessment and Plan:
James was seen today for flank pain.
Diagnoses and all orders for this visit:
Right lateral abdominal pain
-CT Abdomen & Pelvis W IV Contrast Oral Contrast if Indicated by Radiology; Future
 Continue high fiber diet & bowel regiment. Will get CT scan to see if there is abscess or hernia
Patient Instructions
Abdominal pain:
1. Will order a CT or ultrasound of your abdomen - depending on the location of the pain - they will call to schedule
2. Low fat/high fiber diet
3. Small frequent meals
4. If you are constipated, make sure to take miralax daily
5. Go to ER for: fever > 101, severe abdominal pain, blood in stool or vomit 
Return in about 4 weeks (around 2/21/2024) for RLQ abd pain.
Dr. Cassandra Lee, DNP, FNP-C, APRN

nearest emergency department.

Your Medication List as of January 24, 2024 2:53 PM
® Contact your pharmacy to notify of any stopped medications so they can stop any autorefills or charges for
those medications.
levothyroxine 125 MCG tablet
Commonly known as: SYNTHROID
For diagnoses: Hypothyroidism, unspecified type
Take 1 tablet by mouth daily.
Stopped Medication Instructions
Contact your pharmacy to notify of any stopped medications so they can stop any auto-refills or charges for
those medications.
Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.

Clinical Notes
Called to inform patient of PCP message. Was apprecative of this call. 
Pt is calling and states yesterday he was working, cleaning out a house with a friend. Pt is concerned that he may 
have been exposed to a syringe needle whiles cleaning. Pt states the home owner was a diabetic. Pt states he does 
not believe he was pricked or cut by needle and he had on protective gloves. When leaving the house pt states he 
took off his gloves and his hand looked fine but then on the way home, his finger looked like a prick of dry blood *not 
fresh* on his hand. Pt is asking if he should be worried or if she should go get tested. Please advise.
Caller / patient states the following Medical Question: - Pt is calling and states yesterday he was working, cleaning out a house with a friend. Pt is concerned that he may
have been exposed to a syringe needle whiles cleaning. Pt states the home owner was a diabetic. Pt states he does 
not believe he was pricked or cut by needle and he had on protective gloves. When leaving the house pt states he 
took off his gloves and his hand looked fine but then on the way home, his finger looked like a prick of dry blood *not 
fresh* on his hand. Pt is asking if he should be worried or if she should go get tested. Please advise. 
Advised Caller: - a message would be sent to the office with their request.
Caller Expected Outcome: - a MyNortonChart message in reply. - a phone call back.

During the of the visit the patient was educated and counseled about appropriate screening and preventive services. 
The following written plan was given to the patient.
1b. Get a dental checkup annually
2a. Exercise, aim for at least 20 minutes, 3 times a week
4a. Limit alcohol to less than 10 drinks a week. A drink is one beer, 4 ounces of wine, or 1.5 ounces of liquor.
5. Wear you safety belt
6i. Nutrition: look into ways to improve your diet, consider nutrition counseling.
6l. Medications: get help managing your medicine, consider using a weekly pillbox, or having someone help you 
arrange your pills for you.
6n. Sleep: improve your sleep habits, reduce sleeping medications
11b. Over the counter pain medications should be limited. They can cause stomach bleeding, and kidney or liver 
damage.
16b. Screening Test-Colonoscopy soul be preformed at least every 10 years until age 75 (or stool test more 
frequently) for Colorectal cancer. 
16f. Shots- get a Flu vaccine annually.
16j. Shots- get a Tetanus vaccine every 10 years.

Components
Component Value Reference Range Flag Lab
Chlamydia Trachomatis Negative Negative — C
Comment: 
This assay uses transcription mediated amplification to detect ribosomal RNA sequences of Chlamydia trachomatis. Limit 
of detection is 1 inclusion-forming unit per assay for C. trachomatis. Detection of C. trachomatis has a measured 
sensitivity of 94% to
 98% and specificity of 97% to 99%.
This test is FDA approved for in vitro diagnostic testing on cervical, vaginal, and urethral swabs and on male urine 
specimens. Performance on rectal, throat, and female urine specimens has been validated by CPA laboratory.
Neisseria Gonorrhoeae Negative Negative — C
Comment: 
This assay uses transcription mediated amplification to detect ribosomal RNA sequences of Neiseria gonorrhoeae. Limit 
of detection is 50 cells per assay for N. gonorrhoeae. Detection of N. gonorrhoeae has a measured sensitivity of 91% to 
99% and 
specificity of 98% to 99.6%.
This test is FDA approved for in vitro diagnostic testing on cervical, vaginal, and urethral swabs and on male urine

Stopped Medication Instructions
Contact your pharmacy to notify of any stopped medications so they can stop any auto-refills or charges for
those medications.
Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101,0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.

MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit:https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

Group A Strep Source IPOC Throat — — CO41

Group A Strep Comment IPOC (note) — — CO41
Comment: 
This test utilizes real-time polymerase chain reaction to detect a 
DNA sequence unique to Streptococcus pyogenes (Group A 
beta-hemolytic strep) in throat swab specimens collected from 
patients with signs and symptoms of pharyngitis. Diagnostic 
sensitivity has been measured at 98%, specificity at 94%, and limit 
of detection at 5-10 colony forming units per milliliter of 
transport medium. This test does not detect viral or other 
bacterial causes of pharyngitis. If non-Group A Strep bacterial 
causes of pharyngitis are suspected and specific diagnosis is 
warranted, order throat culture. This test has been approved by the 
Food and Drug Administration for use by trained health care 
professionals in laboratories possessing a CLIA waived certificate 
or higher.

Result: 
This test utilizes real-time polymerase chain reaction to detect
RNA sequences unique to SARS-CoV-2 virus, influenza A virus, and
influenza B virus in nasopharyngeal and nasal specimens collected
from patients with symptoms consistent with COVID-19 and/or
influenza. Limit of detection has been measured at 0.012 TCID50/ml
for SARS-CoV-2, 0.003-0.02 TCID50/ml for influenza A, and
0.002-0.004 TCID50/ml for influenza B. This test is currently
available for use under an Emergency Use Authorization from the
Food and Drug Administration.
